commit.message:
feat(agent-studio): v1.2.0 — close 20 PR #118 follow-ups (onboarding scripts, package hardening, validate/scaffold alignment, clean.py rewrite) (#120)

* fix(agent-studio): real onboarding scripts + tighten package.py allow-list

Closes follow-ups OB-1, OB-2, OB-3, PK-1, PK-2 from PR #118.

- OB-1/OB-2: provision.py now mechanically substitutes {{key}} /
  {{config.key}} placeholders in SOUL.md and IDENTITY.md instead of just
  appending a HTML comment; finalize.py reads data/*_baseline.json and
  writes MEMORY.md + data/plan.json instead of TODO stubs. Lift both
  scripts (plus add_cron.py) out of write_onboarding.py's triple-quoted
  string constants into templates/onboarding/*.py — kills the \\{ regex
  escaping and makes the generated code directly lintable.
- OB-3: new add_cron.py mechanically resolves {{config.*}} from
  data/config.json before the agent calls the cron tool, exiting 1 on
  missing keys or delivery fields (silent-misdelivery prevention).
- PK-1: package.py walks agent/ via a strict allow-list (AGENTS.md,
  IDENTITY.md, SOUL.md, BOOTSTRAP.md, HEARTBEAT.md, TOOLS.md) instead of
  rglob — runtime residue (MEMORY.md, USER.md, memory/, helper scripts)
  no longer leaks into the archive.
- PK-2: _clean_agent_file takes has_avatar from the caller; package.py
  no longer copies avatar.png into artifacts/.agents/ as a presence
  marker, so a failed package leaves no working-tree residue.
- automation.md / generated SKILL.md updated to point at the new
  scripts; tightened prose to drop redundant restatements.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(agent-studio): close round-1 review findings

- add_cron.py: treat None/"" as missing, accept 0/False (was rejecting
  valid falsy config values).
- provision.py: exit 1 on unresolved {{...}} placeholders so packs can't
  ship literal template syntax; collect substitutions first and write
  atomically so a partial sub doesn't reach disk before _fail; skip
  personalization when agent/ subdir is present (/test mode in Agent
  Studio) — pollution of Studio's root .md or irreversible mutation of
  the pack template were both wrong; mirror add_cron.py's _fail pattern.
- finalize.py: surface corrupt baseline filenames in the output JSON
  (was silently dropping them); wrap config.json parse with _fail.
- write_onboarding.py: render cron heredoc unindented so the EOF
  terminator stays at column 0 — the previous list-item-indented form
  hung bash.
- package.py: clarify SOUL.md cleaner is legacy backward-compat (current
  provision.py no longer appends the <!-- User preferences --> footer).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(agent-studio): close round-2 review findings

- provision.py: write data/config.json AFTER validation, not before. The
  sentinel was previously persisted before the unresolved-placeholder
  check, leaving a false-complete state on _fail and suppressing
  re-onboarding (codex blocker).
- provision.py: detect /test mode by probing for actual template files
  (agent/SOUL.md or agent/IDENTITY.md), not bare directory existence.
  An empty agent/ — botched install, aborted extract, stale dir — was
  silently disabling personalization (claude code-reviewer).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(agent-studio): v1.2.0 — close 13 more PR #118 follow-ups

Tranche A — validate.py + scaffold.py alignment:
- VA-1: check_crossrefs accepts both skills/<n>/... and .agents/skills/<n>/... refs.
- VA-2: structure check now requires agent/SOUL.md.
- VA-3: scaffold.py initializes cli_dependencies + data_sources sections.
- VA-4: check_skills flags empty/TODO descriptions and empty scripts: [] in manifest entries.
- VA-5: check_listing_assets accepts avatar.png at root OR artifacts/.
- MI-3: check_skills cross-validates disk skills/<dir> against manifest entries.
- MI-5: _skills_section_is_empty handles comment-line-then-list / empty-section cases.

Tranche B — clean.py:
- CL-1: --confirm is now a required gate; refuses to run without it.
- CL-2: phased try/except reports failed_phase + items_cleaned_before_failure.
- CL-3: clean.py no longer writes IDENTITY.md placeholder, so scaffold.py
  can repopulate from discovery args (placeholder was sticky).

Tranche C — small wins:
- GD-1: new shared _list_section_entries; cron_jobs / data_sources parsed with
  strict top-level header match + section-termination.
- GD-2: avatar_url points at avatar.png (archive root) instead of the
  removed artifacts/.agents/ path.
- PK-4: drop cosmetic files_skipped field and second pack_dir.rglob walk.
- DO-1: delivery.md no longer claims Stage 6 never re-archives; share/install
  may invoke package.py when zip/<pack>-<version>.tar.gz is missing.
- DO-2: SKILL.md /studio new and /studio validate use full absolute paths,
  consistent with /studio publish and packaging.md §5e.

Version: 1.1.0 → 1.2.0.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(agent-studio): close round-1 review findings on expansion

- VA-4: lift YAML scalar trim into _common.scalar_value — strip trailing
  inline `# ...` comment before quote-trim. Scaffold emits
  `description: ""  # TODO: fill in Stage 2`; the previous naive
  `.strip('"')` left the value as `# TODO: ...`, defeating the
  empty/TODO check it was added for. validate.py and
  generate_description.py both consume the shared helper now.
- DO-2: bare `scripts/<name>.py` references in
  references/{discovery,testing,automation,packaging}.md rewritten to
  full `.agents/skills/agent-studio/scripts/<name>.py` paths so an LLM
  invoking them from workspace root resolves correctly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor(agent-studio): /simplify pass — consolidate manifest section parsers

Closes round-1 /simplify findings on the v1.2.0 expansion. All three
reviewers (reuse, quality, efficiency) converged on unifying the two
near-duplicate parsers.

- _common.scalar_value: pre-compile the inline-# regex at module scope.
- _common.parse_section_entries: shared YAML list-block parser. Walks
  manifest once per call, captures every sibling scalar via scalar_value
  so callers get consistent quote / inline-comment trim.
- validate.py: _parse_skill_entries + _declared_skill_names deleted;
  check_skills now parses the manifest once and reuses the entries for
  both the disk-vs-manifest cross-check and the empty/TODO description
  + empty scripts checks.
- generate_description.py: _list_section_entries deleted; cron_jobs and
  data_sources use parse_section_entries directly.
- _common.extract_field + extract_multiline_field: delegate quote-trim
  to scalar_value, dropping the duplicated 3-line inline strip.
- validate.py:check_crossrefs: swap loop order so AGENTS.md is split
  once and both path prefixes are checked per line.
- scaffold.py + validate.py: trim narrative comments (cli_dependencies
  / data_sources two-line WHAT, check_structure history note).
- SKILL.md: tighten "Current stage" stage detector — abbreviate the
  repeated `zip/<name>-<version>.tar.gz` / `data/.delivery-state.json`
  references with `$ARCHIVE` / `$STATE` shorthand inside the doc-only
  reasoning text (bash commands stay fully-qualified — DO-2).
- testing.md §4c: fix doc bug — /test mode no longer personalizes
  agent/SOUL.md (provision.py skips placeholder substitution there);
  update the verify step accordingly.

Net: -48 lines, no behavior change. validate + generate_description
both still flag the scaffold-emitted `description: ""  # TODO` and
`scripts: []` placeholders.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* revert(agent-studio): DO-2 — OpenClaw skill paths are skill-relative

DO-2 was a misread. The OpenClaw / Anthropic Skills standard resolves
`scripts/<name>.py` referenced inside a SKILL.md (or its references/)
relative to the SKILL.md's own directory — see office-pack/pptx/SKILL.md,
office-pack/docx/SKILL.md, creator-ops/SKILL.md, and the Anthropic
SKILL_DIR convention. The bare form was correct all along; the original
followup author and this PR's earlier commits both got this wrong.

Revert PR #120's DO-2 rewrites in agent-studio's own SKILL.md and
references/{discovery,testing,automation,packaging}.md back to
skill-relative `scripts/<name>.py`. The install + share sibling
SKILL.md files (which use the workspace-rooted form) are left as-is —
not introduced by this PR, separate decision.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(agent-studio): apply skill-relative script paths across install/share

Continue the DO-2 revert: agent-studio-install/SKILL.md and
agent-studio-share/SKILL.md were using the workspace-rooted
`.agents/skills/<skill>/scripts/<name>.py` form for script
invocations. Convert to skill-relative `scripts/<name>.py`, matching
the OpenClaw / Anthropic Skills standard now used in agent-studio's
own SKILL.md and reference docs.

Cross-skill routing refs (e.g., "route to `.agents/skills/...SKILL.md`"
for /studio share / install) are file-load pointers, not script
invocations — left as-is.

Script invocation conventions now consistent:
- skill-relative `scripts/<name>.py` for Studio-internal scripts
- pack-relative `skills/pack-onboarding/scripts/<name>.py` for scripts
  that ship in deployed packs and run at user onboarding

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(agent-studio): use SKILL_DIR in references/ for script paths

In SKILL.md, bare `scripts/<name>.py` is unambiguous because SKILL.md
and scripts/ are direct siblings under the skill root. In the
references/<file>.md subfiles, scripts/ is at the same depth as
references/ — both are children of the skill root, not of each
other. Bare `scripts/<name>.py` from inside a references/<file>.md
is ambiguous: it could be read as `references/scripts/...` (file-
relative) rather than `../scripts/...` (skill-root-relative).

Use the Anthropic-standard `SKILL_DIR/scripts/<name>.py` placeholder
in references/{discovery,testing,automation,packaging}.md so the path
explicitly anchors at the skill root. Matches the convention seen in
office-pack/xlsx/references/{fix,validate}.md.

SKILL.md keeps bare `scripts/<name>.py` since it sits at the skill
root next to scripts/.

The pack-relative `skills/pack-onboarding/scripts/add_cron.py` line
in automation.md is unrelated — it documents what the deployed child
pack runs at user-onboarding time from the pack root.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(agent-studio): use workspace-rooted full path for script invocations

DO-2 redux — back to the original followups doc's recommendation.

Empirical evidence from a live in-pod E2E test (oc-bundle-test):
when SKILL.md said `uv run --python 3.12 scripts/clean.py ...`, the
studio passed that literally to bash. bash's cwd was the workspace
root (`/home/node/.openclaw/workspace-agent-studio`), where `scripts/`
exists but is empty (the studio scripts actually live at
`.agents/skills/agent-studio/scripts/`). The bare command failed
silently; the studio then ran `find ... -name "clean.py"` to locate
the real path, fell back to manual `rm -f` for cleanup, and only
later discovered the full `.agents/skills/agent-studio/scripts/...`
form by trial-and-error. The CL-1 --confirm gate was bypassed
entirely because clean.py was never actually invoked.

OpenClaw does not auto-resolve `scripts/X.py` relative to the SKILL's
own directory, and there is no `SKILL_DIR` env-var substitution.
The only reliable invocation form is the full workspace-rooted path.

Rewrite all five files:
- SKILL.md  (clean / validate / package)
- references/discovery.md  (scaffold)
- references/testing.md  (validate)
- references/automation.md  (write_onboarding, validate)
- references/packaging.md  (generate_description, package)

…to use `.agents/skills/agent-studio/scripts/<name>.py`. The
pack-relative `skills/pack-onboarding/scripts/...` lines stay as-is
(those execute from the deployed child pack's root, where the
relative path is correct).

Re-tested in the pod: studio now invokes scripts on first try, no
find-and-rm fallback dance.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(agent-studio): introduce ./studio wrapper for short script invocations

`.agents/skills/agent-studio/scripts/<name>.py` is the only filesystem
path that actually resolves from the studio's exec cwd (workspace root)
— bare `scripts/<name>.py` and `SKILL_DIR/scripts/<name>.py` both fail
because OpenClaw doesn't auto-resolve skill-internal paths and doesn't
expose a SKILL_DIR env var. But that full path is long and clutters
every command.

Add a workspace-root `studio` wrapper file (shipped via the bundle,
see openclaw-docker companion commit) that resolves the script path
relative to its own location and execs via `uv run --python 3.12`.
Now SKILL.md and references/<file>.md invoke scripts as
`./studio <name> [args...]`:

  ./studio clean --pack-dir . --confirm
  ./studio validate --pack-dir .
  ./studio scaffold --name X --display-name X ...
  ./studio package --pack-dir . --output zip/

Same five files updated:
- SKILL.md (now also explains the convention)
- references/{discovery,testing,automation,packaging}.md

Re-tested in oc-bundle-test pod: studio invokes scripts on first try
through the wrapper, no find-fallback dance. Bundle wrapper passes
assert_archive_layout (regular file, executable).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Revert "fix(agent-studio): introduce ./studio wrapper for short script invocations"

This reverts commit fb01726162d123919475dc65c8dec7d2bec9de8e.

* fix(agent-studio): use file-relative script paths

- SKILL.md (sibling of scripts/): `scripts/<name>.py`
- references/<x>.md (one level deeper): `../scripts/<name>.py`

Proper filesystem-relative paths from each .md to the target script.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(agent-studio): use <skill_root>/scripts/<name>.py per OpenClaw convention

OpenClaw's skill loader resolves `<skill_root>` as the SKILL.md's
directory when the SKILL.md defines this anchor. Empirically verified
in oc-bundle-test pod: with the Convention block in SKILL.md, studio
resolves `<skill_root>/scripts/clean.py` to the correct absolute path
on first exec — no find-fallback dance.

- SKILL.md: add Convention defining `<skill_root>`, use uniformly
  in /studio new / validate / publish commands.
- references/{discovery,testing,automation,packaging}.md: same form
  (instead of file-relative `../scripts/` which the LLM couldn't
  resolve without the Convention cue).
- Pack-relative `skills/pack-onboarding/scripts/...` lines unchanged.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(agent-studio): apply <skill_root> uniformly in automation.md and generated pack-onboarding SKILL.md

automation.md inconsistency: the cron-section bash heredoc still used
the raw pack-relative form `skills/pack-onboarding/scripts/add_cron.py`
while sibling lines 90 and 114 used `<skill_root>/scripts/<name>.py`.
That snippet is what write_onboarding.py emits into the generated
pack-onboarding SKILL.md — fixing automation.md alone isn't enough.

- automation.md line 54: `<skill_root>/scripts/add_cron.py`
- scripts/write_onboarding.py: emits a Convention block at the top of
  every generated pack-onboarding SKILL.md, then uses
  `<skill_root>/scripts/<name>.py` for add_cron, provision, finalize,
  and scan scripts. In that generated SKILL.md `<skill_root>` resolves
  to `skills/pack-onboarding/` (the pack-onboarding skill's own root).
- templates/onboarding-skill.md.tmpl: same form.

End result: `<skill_root>` is a uniform anchor across every script
reference in agent-studio's docs AND in the generated pack-onboarding
docs. Each skill's own SKILL.md defines what its `<skill_root>`
points to.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(agent-studio): pack-onboarding is simple — bare scripts/<name>.py

Per OpenClaw skill convention: a SKILL.md whose skill has no references/
subdirectory (i.e. SKILL.md and scripts/ are direct siblings) uses bare
`scripts/<name>.py` paths — the LLM auto-resolves against the SKILL.md
directory. `<skill_root>/scripts/...` is only needed when references/
subfiles are at the same depth as scripts/, where bare would be
ambiguous.

pack-onboarding is exactly that simple case (no references/), so:
- write_onboarding.py emits bare `scripts/<name>.py` in the generated
  pack-onboarding SKILL.md (no Convention block needed)
- templates/onboarding-skill.md.tmpl: same
- automation.md line 54: the documented bash snippet now mirrors what
  the generated SKILL.md contains

agent-studio's own SKILL.md keeps `<skill_root>/scripts/<name>.py` +
Convention because it has references/{discovery,testing,automation,
packaging}.md sitting one level deeper than scripts/.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

tree b6c4a3f7e0572fb2b79184a345e75c5e9bd8db00

* fix(agent-studio): apply skill-relative script paths across install/share

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(agent-studio): use SKILL_DIR in references/ for script paths

In SKILL.md, bare `scripts/<name>.py` is unambiguous because SKILL.md
and scripts/ are direct siblings under the skill root. In the
references/<file>.md subfiles, scripts/ is at the same depth as
references/ — both are children of the skill root, not of each
other. Bare `scripts/<name>.py` from inside a references/<file>.md
is ambiguous: it could be read as `references/scripts/...` (file-
relative) rather than `../scripts/...` (skill-root-relative).

Use the Anthropic-standard `SKILL_DIR/scripts/<name>.py` placeholder
in references/{discovery,testing,automation,packaging}.md so the path
explicitly anchors at the skill root. Matches the convention seen in
office-pack/xlsx/references/{fix,validate}.md.

SKILL.md keeps bare `scripts/<name>.py` since it sits at the skill
root next to scripts/.

The pack-relative `skills/pack-onboarding/scripts/add_cron.py` line
in automation.md is unrelated — it documents what the deployed child
pack runs at user-onboarding time from the pack root.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(agent-studio): use workspace-rooted full path for script invocations

DO-2 redux — back to the original followups doc's recommendation.

Empirical evidence from a live in-pod E2E test (oc-bundle-test):
when SKILL.md said `uv run --python 3.12 scripts/clean.py ...`, the
studio passed that literally to bash. bash's cwd was the workspace
root (`/home/node/.openclaw/workspace-agent-studio`), where `scripts/`
exists but is empty (the studio scripts actually live at
`.agents/skills/agent-studio/scripts/`). The bare command failed
silently; the studio then ran `find ... -name "clean.py"` to locate
the real path, fell back to manual `rm -f` for cleanup, and only
later discovered the full `.agents/skills/agent-studio/scripts/...`
form by trial-and-error. The CL-1 --confirm gate was bypassed
entirely because clean.py was never actually invoked.

OpenClaw does not auto-resolve `scripts/X.py` relative to the SKILL's
own directory, and there is no `SKILL_DIR` env-var substitution.
The only reliable invocation form is the full workspace-rooted path.

Rewrite all five files:
- SKILL.md  (clean / validate / package)
- references/discovery.md  (scaffold)
- references/testing.md  (validate)
- references/automation.md  (write_onboarding, validate)
- references/packaging.md  (generate_description, package)

…to use `.agents/skills/agent-studio/scripts/<name>.py`. The
pack-relative `skills/pack-onboarding/scripts/...` lines stay as-is
(those execute from the deployed child pack's root, where the
relative path is correct).

Re-tested in the pod: studio now invokes scripts on first try, no
find-and-rm fallback dance.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(agent-studio): introduce ./studio wrapper for short script invocations

`.agents/skills/agent-studio/scripts/<name>.py` is the only filesystem
path that actually resolves from the studio's exec cwd (workspace root)
— bare `scripts/<name>.py` and `SKILL_DIR/scripts/<name>.py` both fail
because OpenClaw doesn't auto-resolve skill-internal paths and doesn't
expose a SKILL_DIR env var. But that full path is long and clutters
every command.

Add a workspace-root `studio` wrapper file (shipped via the bundle,
see openclaw-docker companion commit) that resolves the script path
relative to its own location and execs via `uv run --python 3.12`.
Now SKILL.md and references/<file>.md invoke scripts as
`./studio <name> [args...]`:

  ./studio clean --pack-dir . --confirm
  ./studio validate --pack-dir .
  ./studio scaffold --name X --display-name X ...
  ./studio package --pack-dir . --output zip/

Same five files updated:
- SKILL.md (now also explains the convention)
- references/{discovery,testing,automation,packaging}.md

Re-tested in oc-bundle-test pod: studio invokes scripts on first try
through the wrapper, no find-fallback dance. Bundle wrapper passes
assert_archive_layout (regular file, executable).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(agent-studio): use <skill_root>/scripts/<name>.py per OpenClaw convention

OpenClaw's skill loader resolves `<skill_root>` as the SKILL.md's
directory when the SKILL.md defines this anchor. Empirically verified
in oc-bundle-test pod: with the Convention block in SKILL.md, studio
resolves `<skill_root>/scripts/clean.py` to the correct absolute path
on first exec — no find-fallback dance.

- SKILL.md: add Convention defining `<skill_root>`, use uniformly
  in /studio new / validate / publish commands.
- references/{discovery,testing,automation,packaging}.md: same form
  (instead of file-relative `../scripts/` which the LLM couldn't
  resolve without the Convention cue).
- Pack-relative `skills/pack-onboarding/scripts/...` lines unchanged.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(agent-studio): apply <skill_root> uniformly in automation.md and generated pack-onboarding SKILL.md

automation.md inconsistency: the cron-section bash heredoc still used
the raw pack-relative form `skills/pack-onboarding/scripts/add_cron.py`
while sibling lines 90 and 114 used `<skill_root>/scripts/<name>.py`.
That snippet is what write_onboarding.py emits into the generated
pack-onboarding SKILL.md — fixing automation.md alone isn't enough.

- automation.md line 54: `<skill_root>/scripts/add_cron.py`
- scripts/write_onboarding.py: emits a Convention block at the top of
  every generated pack-onboarding SKILL.md, then uses
  `<skill_root>/scripts/<name>.py` for add_cron, provision, finalize,
  and scan scripts. In that generated SKILL.md `<skill_root>` resolves
  to `skills/pack-onboarding/` (the pack-onboarding skill's own root).
- templates/onboarding-skill.md.tmpl: same form.

End result: `<skill_root>` is a uniform anchor across every script
reference in agent-studio's docs AND in the generated pack-onboarding
docs. Each skill's own SKILL.md defines what its `<skill_root>`
points to.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(agent-studio): pack-onboarding is simple — bare scripts/<name>.py

Per OpenClaw skill convention: a SKILL.md whose skill has no references/
subdirectory (i.e. SKILL.md and scripts/ are direct siblings) uses bare
`scripts/<name>.py` paths — the LLM auto-resolves against the SKILL.md
directory. `<skill_root>/scripts/...` is only needed when references/
subfiles are at the same depth as scripts/, where bare would be
ambiguous.

pack-onboarding is exactly that simple case (no references/), so:
- write_onboarding.py emits bare `scripts/<name>.py` in the generated
  pack-onboarding SKILL.md (no Convention block needed)
- templates/onboarding-skill.md.tmpl: same
- automation.md line 54: the documented bash snippet now mirrors what
  the generated SKILL.md contains

agent-studio's own SKILL.md keeps `<skill_root>/scripts/<name>.py` +
Convention because it has references/{discovery,testing,automation,
packaging}.md sitting one level deeper than scripts/.
